reasoning: The event category 'Explosions/Remote violence' tracks large-scale explosive and remote weaponry use (e.g., airstrikes, IEDs, shelling), which virtually never occur in Switzerland. Even ATM bombings are either too small to be recorded by ACLED in this way or simply do not cross the threshold, considering the prior 360-day baseline provided by ACLED itself was zero events (making the reference 1.000). To trigger a YES resolution, Switzerland would need to experience more than 10 times this baseline (i.e. strictly greater than 10, meaning at least 11 events) in a narrow 30-day window. There is no plausible indication of such an extreme spike in destructive violence occurring in Switzerland over the coming months. Therefore, the probability is set to the lower bounds allowed.
